title: symphytum × bicknellii
intro: Symphytum × bicknellii, also known as comfrey, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family. Its medicinal properties lie in its leaves and roots, which contain allantoin, a compound that promotes wound healing and tissue repair. Comfrey is valued for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating joint pain due to its anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties. Comfrey can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, salves, and tinctures, which are often used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds, or internally to support digestive health.
